revision.comment: 新しいページ：横塚古墳（栃木県下野市にあった古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 横塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山522 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=横塚古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長52m
高さ5.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=横塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=30.90|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=17.55|mark=前方後円墳-西北西.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''横塚古墳'''（よこつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:横塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、姿川左岸の台地縁辺に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。かつては周辺に多くの古墳が存在したというが、現在は数基のみが遺存する。本古墳もかつて旧石橋中学校校庭に存在したが、現在では失われている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けた。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（人物・靫形・大刀形・弓形・馬形埴輪など）が検出されている{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf 横塚古墳]}}（下野市）。。また墳丘周囲には幅約10-17メートルの周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては70メートルにおよぶ。埋葬施設は前方部のくびれ部寄りにおける[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口した。石室全長は約11メートルを測り、玄室・前室・羨道から構成される大型の複室構造の石室である[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。石室の奥壁・天井石には[[凝灰岩]]（大谷石）が使用され、側壁は川原石の小口積みによって構築される。石室内からは副葬品として装身具（金環・銀環・銅環・金銅製刀装具）・武器武具（直刀・刀子・鉄鏃・[[挂甲]]小札）・馬具（鞍の磯金具・雲珠）・[[須恵器]]（高坏・甕）などが出土している。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。 石室内からの出土品は[[東京国立博物館]]に送られ、現地では石室奥壁とされる石材のみが遺存する。 === 来歴 === * [[明治]]期、発掘。横穴式石室の確認史跡説明板。。 * [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）、石室内出土品を帝室博物館（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）に寄贈。 * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、旧石橋中学校の校庭拡張に伴う発掘調査。 * [[1957年]]（昭和32年）、整地工事により消滅。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約52メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約32メートル ** 高さ：約5.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約37メートル ** 高さ：約4.5メートル == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 横塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（下野市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=横塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:よこつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 横塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域） |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山522 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=横塚古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長52m
高さ5.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=横塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=30.90|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=17.55|mark=前方後円墳-西北西.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''横塚古墳'''（よこつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）を構成した古墳の1つ。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |'''横塚古墳'''||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長52m||6c後半||（消滅） |- |[[御鷲山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m||6c後半||なし |- |[[下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長84m||6c末||（消滅） |- |[[多功大塚山古墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺54m||7c中||町史跡 |}
[[ファイル:横塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、姿川左岸の台地縁辺に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。かつては周辺に多くの古墳が存在したというが、現在は数基のみが遺存する。本古墳もかつて旧石橋中学校校庭に存在したが、現在では失われている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けた。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（人物・靫形・大刀形・弓形・馬形埴輪など）が検出されている{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf 横塚古墳]}}（下野市）。。また墳丘周囲には幅約10-17メートルの周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては70メートルにおよぶ。埋葬施設は前方部のくびれ部寄りにおける[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口した。石室全長は約11メートルを測り、玄室・前室・羨道から構成される大型の複室構造の石室である[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。石室の奥壁・天井石には[[凝灰岩]]（大谷石）が使用され、側壁は川原石の小口積みによって構築される。石室内からは副葬品として装身具（金環・銀環・銅環・金銅製刀装具）・武器武具（直刀・刀子・鉄鏃・[[挂甲]]小札）・馬具（鞍の磯金具・雲珠）・[[須恵器]]（高坏・甕）などが出土している。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。 石室内からの出土品は[[東京国立博物館]]に送られ、現地では石室奥壁とされる石材のみが遺存する。 === 来歴 === * [[明治]]期、発掘。横穴式石室の確認史跡説明板。。 * [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）、石室内出土品を帝室博物館（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）に寄贈。 * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、旧石橋中学校の校庭拡張に伴う発掘調査。 * [[1957年]]（昭和32年）、整地工事により消滅。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約52メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約32メートル ** 高さ：約5.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約37メートル ** 高さ：約4.5メートル == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 横塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（下野市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=横塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:よこつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

高さ5.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=横塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=30.90|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=17.55|mark=前方後円墳-西北西.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''横塚古墳'''（よこつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち石橋・薬師寺地域）を構成した古墳の1つ。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |'''横塚古墳'''||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長52m||6c後半||（消滅） |- |[[御鷲山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m||6c後半||なし |- |[[下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長84m||6c末||（消滅） |- |[[多功大塚山古墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺54m||7c中||町史跡 |}
[[ファイル:横塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、姿川左岸の台地縁辺に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。かつては周辺に多くの古墳が存在したというが、現在は数基のみが遺存する。本古墳もかつて旧石橋中学校校庭に存在したが、現在では失われている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けた。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（人物・靫形・大刀形・弓形・馬形埴輪など）が検出されている{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf 横塚古墳]}}（下野市）。。また墳丘周囲には幅約10-17メートルの周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては70メートルにおよぶ。埋葬施設は前方部のくびれ部寄りにおける[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口した。石室全長は約11メートルを測り、玄室・前室・羨道から構成される大型の複室構造の石室である[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。石室の奥壁・天井石には[[凝灰岩]]（大谷石）が使用され、側壁は川原石の小口積みによって構築される。石室内からは副葬品として装身具（金環・銀環・銅環・金銅製刀装具）・武器武具（直刀・刀子・鉄鏃・[[挂甲]]小札）・馬具（鞍の磯金具・雲珠）・[[須恵器]]（高坏・甕）などが出土している。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。 石室内からの出土品は[[東京国立博物館]]に送られ、現地では石室奥壁とされる石材のみが遺存する。 === 来歴 === * [[明治]]期、発掘。横穴式石室の確認史跡説明板。。 * [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）、石室内出土品を帝室博物館（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）に寄贈。 * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、旧石橋中学校の校庭拡張に伴う発掘調査。 * [[1957年]]（昭和32年）、整地工事により消滅。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約52メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約32メートル ** 高さ：約5.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約37メートル ** 高さ：約4.5メートル == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 横塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（下野市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=横塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:よこつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 横塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域） |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山522 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=横塚古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長52m
高さ5.4m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=横塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=26|lat_sec=30.90|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=17.55|mark=前方後円墳-西北西.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|25|18|N|139|51|42|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 250| height =320 | caption = {{center|しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(横塚) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=御鷲山 | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=下石橋愛宕塚 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=多功大塚山 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=下野薬師寺跡 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''横塚古墳'''（よこつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち石橋・薬師寺地域）を構成した古墳の1つ。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |'''横塚古墳'''||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長52m||6c後半||（消滅） |- |[[御鷲山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m||6c後半||なし |- |[[下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長84m||6c末||（消滅） |- |[[多功大塚山古墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺54m||7c中||町史跡 |}
[[ファイル:横塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、姿川左岸の台地縁辺に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。かつては周辺に多くの古墳が存在したというが、現在は数基のみが遺存する。本古墳もかつて旧石橋中学校校庭に存在したが、現在では失われている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けた。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（人物・靫形・大刀形・弓形・馬形埴輪など）が検出されている{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf 横塚古墳]}}（下野市）。。また墳丘周囲には幅約10-17メートルの周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては70メートルにおよぶ。埋葬施設は前方部のくびれ部寄りにおける[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口した。石室全長は約11メートルを測り、玄室・前室・羨道から構成される大型の複室構造の石室である[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。石室の奥壁・天井石には[[凝灰岩]]（大谷石）が使用され、側壁は川原石の小口積みによって構築される。石室内からは副葬品として装身具（金環・銀環・銅環・金銅製刀装具）・武器武具（直刀・刀子・鉄鏃・[[挂甲]]小札）・馬具（鞍の磯金具・雲珠）・[[須恵器]]（高坏・甕）などが出土している。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。 石室内からの出土品は[[東京国立博物館]]に送られ、現地では石室奥壁とされる石材のみが遺存する。 === 来歴 === * [[明治]]期、発掘。横穴式石室の確認史跡説明板。。 * [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）、石室内出土品を帝室博物館（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）に寄贈。 * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、旧石橋中学校の校庭拡張に伴う発掘調査。 * [[1957年]]（昭和32年）、整地工事により消滅。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約52メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約32メートル ** 高さ：約5.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約37メートル ** 高さ：約4.5メートル == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 横塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 史跡説明板（下野市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=横塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=横塚古墳|title=石橋町史 第1巻 史料編 上|publisher=石橋町|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:よこつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(横塚) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=御鷲山 | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=下石橋愛宕塚 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=多功大塚山 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=下野薬師寺跡 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''横塚古墳'''（よこつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち石橋・薬師寺地域）を構成した古墳の1つ。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |'''横塚古墳'''||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長52m||6c後半||（消滅） |- |[[御鷲山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m||6c後半||なし |- |[[下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長84m||6c末||（消滅） |- |[[多功大塚山古墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺54m||7c中||町史跡 |}
[[ファイル:横塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、姿川左岸の台地縁辺に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。かつては周辺に多くの古墳が存在したというが、現在は数基のみが遺存する。本古墳もかつて旧石橋中学校校庭に存在したが、現在では失われている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けた。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（人物・靫形・大刀形・弓形・馬形埴輪など）が検出されている{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf 横塚古墳]}}（下野市）。。また墳丘周囲には幅約10-17メートルの周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては70メートルにおよぶ。埋葬施設は前方部のくびれ部寄りにおける[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口した。石室全長は約11メートルを測り、玄室・前室・羨道から構成される大型の複室構造の石室である[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。石室の奥壁・天井石には[[凝灰岩]]（大谷石）が使用され、側壁は川原石の小口積みによって構築される。石室内からは副葬品として装身具（金環・銀環・銅環・金銅製刀装具）・武器武具（直刀・刀子・鉄鏃・[[挂甲]]小札）・馬具（鞍の磯金具・雲珠）・[[須恵器]]（高坏・甕）などが出土している。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。 石室内からの出土品は[[東京国立博物館]]に送られ、現地では石室奥壁とされる石材のみが遺存する。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[明治]]期、発掘。横穴式石室の確認史跡説明板。。 * [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）、石室内出土品を帝室博物館（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）に寄贈。 * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、旧石橋中学校の校庭拡張に伴う発掘調査。 * [[1957年]]（昭和32年）、整地工事により消滅。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約52メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約32メートル ** 高さ：約5.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約37メートル ** 高さ：約4.5メートル == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 横塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 史跡説明板（下野市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=横塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=横塚古墳|title=石橋町史 第1巻 史料編 上|publisher=石橋町|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:よこつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(横塚) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=御鷲山 | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=下石橋愛宕塚 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=多功大塚山 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=下野薬師寺跡 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''横塚古墳'''（よこつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]下古山にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち石橋・薬師寺地域）を構成した古墳のひとつ。現在では[[墳丘]]は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |'''横塚古墳'''||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長52m||6c後半||（消滅） |- |[[御鷲山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m||6c後半||なし |- |[[下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長84m||6c末||（消滅） |- |[[多功大塚山古墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺54m||7c中||町史跡 |}
栃木県南部、姿川左岸の台地縁辺に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。かつては周辺に多くの古墳が存在したというが、現在は数基のみが遺存する。本古墳もかつて旧[[下野市立石橋中学校]]校庭に存在したが、現在では失われている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（人物・靫形・大刀形・弓形・馬形埴輪など）が検出されている{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf 横塚古墳]}}（下野市）。。また墳丘周囲には幅約10 - 17[[メートル|m]]の周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては70mに及ぶ。 埋葬施設は前方部のくびれ部寄りにおける[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長は約11mを測り、玄室・前室・羨道から構成される大型の複室構造の石室である[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳] 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム。石室の奥壁・天井石には[[凝灰岩]]（大谷石）が使用され、側壁は川原石の小口積みによって構築される。 石室内からは副葬品として、装身具（金環・銀環・銅環・金銅製刀装具）、武器武具（直刀・刀子・鉄鏃・[[挂甲]]小札）、馬具（鞍の磯金具・雲珠）、[[須恵器]]（高坏・甕）などが出土している。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。 石室内からの出土品は[[東京国立博物館]]に送られ、現地では石室奥壁とされる石材のみが遺存する。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[明治]]期、発掘。横穴式石室の確認史跡説明板。。 * [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）、石室内出土品を帝室博物館（現在の[[東京国立博物館]]）に寄贈。 * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、旧[[下野市立石橋中学校]]の校庭拡張に伴う発掘調査。 * [[1957年]]（昭和32年）、整地工事により消滅。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約52メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約32メートル ** 高さ：約5.4メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約37メートル ** 高さ：約4.5メートル == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 横塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 {{-}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=横塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|横塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} * 史跡説明板（下野市教育委員会設置） === 関連文献 === : 記事執筆に使用していない関連文献 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=横塚古墳|title=石橋町史 第1巻 史料編 上|publisher=石橋町|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[御鷲山古墳]] / [[下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] - しもつけ古墳群 * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 横塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=栃木県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
{{Confused|Yokotsukayama Kofun}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Yokotsuka Kofun | native_name = 横塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Yokotsuka Kofun Artifacts.JPG|250px|right|Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun]] | image_caption = Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun
Exhibited at Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' (横塚古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|small-scale armor|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: approx. 32 meters * Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: * Width: approx. 37 meters * Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[ja:横塚古墳]]

It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|small-scale armor|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: approx. 32 meters * Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: * Width: approx. 37 meters * Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|small-scale armor|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: approx. 32 meters * Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: * Width: approx. 37 meters * Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Confused|Yokotsukayama Kofun}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Yokotsuka Kofun | native_name = 横塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Yokotsuka Kofun Artifacts.JPG|250px|right|Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun]] | image_caption = Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun
Exhibited at Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' (横塚古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|small-scale armor|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Yokotzuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Yokotzuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Yokotzuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Yokotzuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:横塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Confused|Yokotsukayama Kofun}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Yokotsuka Kofun | native_name = 横塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Yokotsuka Kofun Artifacts.JPG|250px|right|Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun]] | image_caption = Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun
Exhibited at Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' (横塚古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|small-scale armor|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|small-scale armor|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Confused|Yokotsukayama Kofun}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Yokotsuka Kofun | native_name = 横塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Yokotsuka Kofun Artifacts.JPG|250px|right|Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun]] | image_caption = Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun
Exhibited at Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' (横塚古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|small-scale armor|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|small-scale armor|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Confused|Yokotsukayama Kofun}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Yokotsuka Kofun | native_name = 横塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Yokotsuka Kofun Artifacts.JPG|250px|right|Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun]] | image_caption = Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun
Exhibited at Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' (横塚古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Confused|Yokotsukayama Kofun}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Yokotsuka Kofun | native_name = 横塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:Yokotsuka Kofun Artifacts.JPG|250px|right|Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun]] | image_caption = Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun
Exhibited at Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' (横塚古墳) was a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum.
