This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High revision.comment: Text replacement - "Zenpokoenfun" to "Zenpōkōenfun"
revision.text:
[[ja:横塚古墳]] [[ja:横塚古墳]] [[ja:横塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.441916666666664|N|139.8548888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Confused|Yokotsukayama Kofun}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Yokotsuka Kofun | native_name = 横塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:横塚古墳 出土品.JPG|250px|right|burial goods from Yokotsuka Kofun]] | image_caption = Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun
Exhibited at Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum | type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | designation1 = }} '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' (横塚古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515|1=Shimotsuke Kofun Group}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owashiyama Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}|| Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owashiyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|横塚古墳|version=101074807|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:横塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q114497967}} [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.441916666666664|N|139.8548888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Confused|Yokotsukayama Kofun}} {{Infobox historic site | name = Yokotsuka Kofun | native_name = 横塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = [[File:横塚古墳 出土品.JPG|250px|right|burial goods from Yokotsuka Kofun]] | image_caption = Artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun
Exhibited at Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | designation1 = }} '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' (横塚古墳) was a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515|1=Shimotsuke Kofun Group}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region). The mound no longer exists. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | '''Yokotsuka Kofun''' || {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owashiyama Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}|| Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
The kofun was constructed on the edge of a plateau along the left bank of the Sugata River in southern Tochigi Prefecture{{Sfn|Yokotsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988}}. Several mounds once existed in the area, but only a few remain today. This particular kofun was located within the former grounds of the old {{ill|Ishibashi Junior High School|ja|下野市立石橋中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashi Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but the mound has been completely lost. The mound was keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west. Investigations revealed {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, house-shaped haniwa, and other shaped haniwa (including representations of humans, quivers, swords, bows, and horses) on the mound’s surface.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/manage/contents/upload/5821842675067.pdf Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun]}} - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. A surrounding moat, 10–17 meters wide, encircled the mound, and the total diameter, including the moat, measured approximately 70 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located near the constriction between the mound's front and back sections, opening southward. The stone chamber measured approximately 11 meters in total length, featuring a multi-chamber structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage. Tuff stone blocks (Ōya stone) were used for the back wall and ceiling, while the side walls were constructed from river stones arranged in a "small-mouth" style. Artifacts excavated from the chamber include ornamental items (gold, silver, and bronze rings; gilt bronze fittings for swords), weapons and armor (straight swords, small knives, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Lamellar armour|lt=Keikō (lamellar armour)|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} plates), horse trappings (metal fittings for saddles, pendants), and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} such as tall cups and jars. The mound is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Artifacts recovered from the chamber were transferred to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. Only stones from the back wall remain on-site. == Historical Timeline == * Meiji period: Excavation revealed the presence of a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}.Historical Site Information Board. * 1908: Artifacts from the chamber were donated to the Imperial Museum (now Tokyo National Museum). * 1953: Excavation conducted during the expansion of Ishibashi Junior High School's grounds. * 1957: The site was leveled during land development. == Mound Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Mound length: approx. 52 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approx. 32 meters ** Height: approx. 5.4 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: approx. 37 meters ** Height: approx. 4.5 meters == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142|1=Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Material Museum}} (Shimotsuke City) – Houses and displays artifacts from Yokotsuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 9 Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490093}} * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Shimotsuke City Board of Education) === Additional Literature === * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yokotsuka Kofun|title=Ishibashi Town History, Volume 1, Historical Materials|publisher=Ishibashi Town|isbn=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owashiyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=41 Yokotsuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual
